content.problemStatement:
To access CRED programs, one needs to have a credit score of 750750750 or more.

Given that the present credit score is XXX, determine if one can access CRED programs or not.

If it is possible to access CRED programs, output YES\texttt{YES}YES, otherwise output NO\texttt{NO}NO.


Input Format
The first and only line of input contains a single integer XXX, the credit score.



Output Format
Print YES\texttt{YES}YES if it is possible to access CRED programs. Otherwise, print NO\texttt{NO}NO.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YeS\texttt{YeS}YeS, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, yes\texttt{yes}yes and YES\texttt{YES}YES will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the credit score for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a line containing "YES" if X ≥ 750, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: 823 ≥ 750 → YES. Second test case: 251 < 750 → NO.
750 is exactly the threshold, so the answer is YES.
0 and 749 are below 750 → NO. 10000 is above 750 → YES.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read X and compare it with the threshold 750. If X is greater than or equal to 750 print YES, otherwise print NO. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
